name: QRadar-0006 - Windows Privilege Escalation Detection
description: Detects privilege escalation attempts on Windows systems by monitoring for suspicious use of built-in elevation tools, token impersonation, UAC bypass techniques, and unexpected additions to privileged groups. Correlates multiple escalation indicators for higher-confidence alerting.

mitre.technique_name: Abuse Elevation Control Mechanism: Bypass User Account Control

detection.log_source_type: Windows Security Event Log, Windows System Event Log, Sysmon
detection.event_name_filter: Event ID 4728, 4732, 4756 (Group membership), Event ID 4672 (Special logon), Event ID 4648 (Explicit credentials)
detection.aql: SELECT username, sourceIP, EventID, CATEGORYNAME(category) as event_category, COUNT(*) as event_count FROM events WHERE LOGSOURCETYPENAME(devicetype) ILIKE '%windows%' AND (EventID IN (4728, 4732, 4756, 4672) OR (EventID = 4688 AND (UTF8(payload) ILIKE '%whoami /priv%' OR UTF8(payload) ILIKE '%icacls%' OR UTF8(payload) ILIKE '%net localgroup administrators%' OR UTF8(payload) ILIKE '%runas%' OR UTF8(payload) ILIKE '%bypassuac%' OR UTF8(payload) ILIKE '%eventvwr%' OR UTF8(payload) ILIKE '%fodhelper%'))) GROUP BY username, sourceIP, EventID, event_category HAVING COUNT(*) >= 1 LAST 15 MINUTES

response.offense_description: Suspicious privilege escalation activity detected. User account may have been added to a privileged group, or UAC bypass technique was attempted.

notes: Inspired by SIGMA rules: win_susp_uac_bypass and win_susp_local_group_membership_add. Requires Windows Security Audit Policy to log process creation and group membership changes. Deploy Sysmon for enhanced process-level visibility.
author: Security Team
